steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The equation $H = 2.403L + 23.31$ is linear. The question asks for the best interpretation of the coefficient 2.403 in context. In a linear model $H = mL + b$, the slope $m$ is the change in $H$ per one-unit increase in $L$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "increase in estimated height for each 1-inch increase in femur length": Slope = rise/run = change in $H$ per change in $L$. This is exactly what 2.403 represents. ✓
  • Choice B — "increase in femur length for each 1-inch increase in estimated height": Reverses the roles of $H$ and $L$. The slope gives $ΔH/ΔL$, not $ΔL/ΔH$. ✗
  • Choice C — "increase in femur length for each 23.31-inch increase in estimated height": Confuses slope with the $y$-intercept and still reverses the variables. ✗
  • Choice D — "estimated height of a student whose femur has length $L$ inches": That description fits the entire expression $H$, not the coefficient 2.403 alone. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
